1_prerequisite_chain.description: Each chapter's required prior knowledge and where it was taught

Ch01.note: First chapter - no prior chapter dependency. Explicitly states external prerequisites.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch01에서 버튼 인터럽트와 LED 제어 코드를 완성했습니다

Ch02.note: Correctly references Ch01 achievement in opening paragraph. LOG macros become universal infrastructure.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch02에서 LOG_I 매크로로 MCU 내부를 들여다보는 데 성공했습니다

Ch03.note: Opens with achievement validation. Maps Ch01-02 code onto 4-layer diagram. Restaurant metaphor introduced here, reused in Ch05.

Ch04.note: Excellent contrast: SWO (Ch02) = debugger-only stethoscope vs UART = universal voice. First Driver layer component.

Ch05.note: Builds directly on Ch04 polling problem. Reuses Ch03 restaurant metaphor (chef = CPU, serving robot = DMA). Excellent motivation chain.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch05에서 우리는 이 문제의 해결사를 이미 만났습니다
Ch03에서 설계한 레이어드 아키텍처의 최강 실증

Ch06.note: STRONGEST architecture validation point: same API (Ch04), completely different internals (DMA). Ring buffer introduced here, reused in Ch15 CLI.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch01에서 LED를 깜빡일 때 이 함수를 사용했죠
Ch04에서 uart_driver를 만들었던 것과 동일한 패턴

Ch07.note: HAL_Delay() as 'prison' callback to Ch01. tim_driver follows uart_driver pattern from Ch04. Good pattern reuse.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch07에서 TIM2로 정밀 주기 타이머와 PWM을 완성

Ch08.note: Critical bridge chapter: Ch03 FSM theory -> Ch08 FSM practice. Service layer transitions from theoretical (Ch03) to real (stopwatch_service). Metacognition box checks Ch07 and Ch03 prerequisites.

prerequisites_from_prior.concept: ISR flag + main loop FSM pattern
prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: ISR에서 플래그만 설정하고, main loop에서 FSM을 처리하는 이유

Ch09.note: PATTERN REUSE masterclass: Alarm FSM = identical structure to Stopwatch FSM from Ch08. Student feels 'I've seen this before' = pattern internalization. Metacognition explicitly checks Ch08 prerequisites.

Ch10.note: MILESTONE 1: First complete integrated application. Composes RTC (Ch09) + Motor (new) + GPIO (Ch01) + Architecture (Ch03). Math taught before application (gearbox -> excitation -> integration).

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: Ch09의 RTC Alarm FSM 패턴을 재활용
I2C가 '직렬' 통신인 UART와 어떤 차이가 있을까요?

Ch11.note: 3rd FSM application (Sensor Service). Triple-layer driver pattern (I2C Driver -> SHT31 Device Driver -> Sensor Service). CRC-8 is new math concept, taught with analogy before code.

prerequisites_from_prior.reference_in_text: SPI DMA based on Ch05 DMA knowledge

Ch12.note: Completes the smart watch stack. SPI contrasted with I2C (Ch11) - '4-lane highway' analogy. DMA reused from Ch05. LCD = 'the system gets eyes'.

2_code_reuse_verification.description: Tracing specific code/concept reuse chains across chapters

chains.name: LOG macros (universal infrastructure)
chains.chain: Ch02 (LOG_E/W/I/D defined) -> Ch04~Ch12 (all code examples use LOG_* macros)
chains.evidence: Every code example from Ch04 onward includes LOG_D/LOG_I calls per CLAUDE.md requirement

chains.name: 4-layer architecture (HAL/Driver/Service/App)
chains.chain: Ch03 (theory + v0.3 blueprint) -> Ch04 (Driver first: uart_driver) -> Ch07 (Driver: tim_driver) -> Ch08 (Service first: stopwatch_service) -> Ch09 (parallel add: alarm_service + rtc_driver) -> Ch10 (compose: clock_service + stepper_driver) -> Ch11 (triple: i2c_driver + sht31_driver + sensor_service) -> Ch12 (spi_driver + ili9341_driver)
chains.evidence: Architecture SVG updated every chapter from Ch04 onward (ch04_sec05, ch05_sec05, ch06_sec05, ch07_sec06, ch08_sec8, ch09_sec8, ch10_sec08, ch12_sec08)

chains.name: FSM pattern progression
chains.chain: Ch03 (theory: 4 elements) -> Ch08 (1st practice: Stopwatch FSM) -> Ch09 (2nd: Alarm FSM, same structure) -> Ch10 (3rd: Motor FSM) -> Ch11 (4th: Sensor Service FSM)
chains.evidence: Ch08 introduces, Ch09 explicitly says 'same pattern', Ch10/Ch11 continue pattern. 176 FSM mentions across 8 chapters.

chains.name: Ring buffer
chains.chain: Ch02 (async log concept mentioned) -> Ch06 (ring_buffer.h/c implementation) -> Ch15 (planned: CLI input processing)
chains.evidence: 92 ring buffer mentions across 5 chapters. Ch06 implements, TABLE_OF_CONTENTS confirms Ch15 reuse.

chains.name: Driver pattern (init/start/stop/set)
chains.chain: Ch04 (uart_driver: first) -> Ch07 (tim_driver: same pattern) -> Ch09 (rtc_driver) -> Ch10 (stepper_driver) -> Ch11 (i2c_driver + sht31_driver) -> Ch12 (spi_driver + ili9341_driver)
chains.evidence: Ch07 explicitly states 'Ch04에서 uart_driver를 만들었던 것과 동일한 패턴'

chains.name: Restaurant/kitchen metaphor
chains.chain: Ch03 (introduced: restaurant 4-layer analogy) -> Ch05 (reused: chef=CPU, serving robot=DMA) -> Ch06 (referenced: architecture encapsulation proof)
chains.evidence: 22 metaphor mentions across 5 chapters. Consistent reuse strengthens mental model.

chains.name: DMA concept progression
chains.chain: Ch05 (DMA theory + Mem-to-Mem) -> Ch06 (DMA + UART) -> Ch12 (DMA + SPI LCD) -> Ch14 (planned: DMA + I2C)
chains.evidence: Each chapter applies DMA to a new peripheral, building on the same foundation

chains.name: Communication protocol comparison
chains.chain: Ch02 (SWO: debug-only) -> Ch04 (UART: universal async serial) -> Ch11 (I2C: multi-device bus) -> Ch12 (SPI: high-speed point-to-point)
chains.evidence: Each new protocol explicitly contrasted with previous ones. Ch12 has I2C vs SPI comparison table.

3_version_capability_matrix.description: What each version adds to the running project

4_cognitive_load_analysis.description: New concepts per chapter and abstraction jumps

Ch03.note: Intentionally limited to 3 new concepts per review feedback

Ch08.note: Highest new concept count, but FSM theory was pre-taught in Ch03

Ch09.note: FSM pattern reuse reduces effective cognitive load

peak.mitigated_by: Ch03 FSM pre-teaching + Ch07 timer foundation

4_cognitive_load_analysis.assessment: Well-managed. Ch08 is the only spike (5 concepts), but FSM theory was pre-taught in Ch03, reducing effective load. After Ch08, all chapters stay at 3 new concepts.

arc.trigger: First LED blink, first hardware interaction
Can see inside MCU via logs
Architecture theory feels abstract, but restaurant metaphor grounds it
MCU 'speaks' to PC for the first time
DMA is complex, but performance numbers are dramatic (87K cycles saved)
Same API, completely different internals = architecture proof
Math formulas cause anxiety, but LED brightness control = immediate reward
Most complex chapter, but first real FSM + Service layer = breakthrough moment
'I've seen this pattern before' feeling = internalization
Motor math is complex, but seeing motor actually move by RTC time = peak satisfaction
New protocol (I2C), but driver pattern is familiar. CRC-8 is new math.
'The system gets eyes' - LCD display completes the smart watch ecosystem

5_emotional_arc.overall_pattern: Wave pattern: Low(Ch01-02) -> Medium(Ch03-06) -> High peak(Ch07-08) -> Relief(Ch09) -> High peak(Ch10) -> Medium(Ch11) -> Climax(Ch12)
5_emotional_arc.assessment: Excellent emotional design. Anxiety peaks (Ch08, Ch10) are immediately followed by relief/confidence chapters (Ch09, Ch11). Ch12 is the emotional climax for Part 0-3.

6_skill_scaffolding_verification.description: Verifying that theory/math is taught before application

scaffolding_chains.note: Long gap (5 chapters), but Ch08 metacognition box explicitly reminds students. Gap is intentional - students need Driver experience first.
Math taught within same chapter, before code. Sections ordered: theory -> driver -> integration.

6_skill_scaffolding_verification.assessment: All theory precedes application. The Ch03->Ch08 FSM gap (5 chapters) is the longest, but it is intentional and mitigated by metacognition reminders.

7_prerequisite_gaps_found.description: Any missing or weak prerequisite connections

gaps.issue: Ch11 badge says 'v0.9 -> v1.1' skipping v1.0. This is because Ch10 is v1.0 and Ch11 builds on the overall project, but the version notation in Ch11 header is slightly confusing.
gaps.impact: Student might wonder if they missed v1.0. The text body does reference Ch10's achievements.
gaps.recommendation: No action needed - the text clarifies the progression.

gaps.issue: Ch12 does not have a '스스로 점검' (metacognition) box in the introduction, unlike Ch08/Ch09/Ch11.
gaps.impact: Low. Ch12 has architecture position section that serves a similar purpose.
gaps.recommendation: Consider adding a metacognition box checking DMA (Ch05) and I2C vs SPI understanding.

8_cross_chapter_reference_density.description: Number of cross-chapter references per chapter (higher = better integration)
8_cross_chapter_reference_density.assessment: Ch06 has highest density (49) - expected as it integrates Ch04+Ch05. Ch12 has lowest density (13) for later chapters - could benefit from more explicit back-references to DMA (Ch05) and architecture (Ch03).

strengths: Every chapter opens with cumulative achievement acknowledgment ('여기까지 온 여러분의 성과를 확인합시다')
FSM pattern taught once (Ch03 theory, Ch08 first practice), then reused 3 more times (Ch09, Ch10, Ch11) - perfect scaffolding
Architecture diagram updated every chapter from Ch04 - visual continuity
Restaurant metaphor (Ch03) consistently reused through Ch06 - anchors abstract concepts
Driver pattern (init/start/stop/set) established in Ch04, replicated in Ch07/Ch09/Ch10/Ch11/Ch12
Emotional wave pattern prevents fatigue: high peaks (Ch08, Ch10, Ch12) alternated with consolidation (Ch09, Ch11)
Metacognition boxes in Ch08/Ch09/Ch11 explicitly check prior prerequisites
Version roadmap (v0.1~v1.2) gives tangible progress markers - students see accumulation
Each version genuinely adds capability (not just API refresh) - verified through code examples
weaknesses: Ch12 has lowest cross-reference density (13) among later chapters - could be more connected
Ch12 lacks metacognition self-check box unlike Ch08/Ch09/Ch11
9_summary.verdict: EXCELLENT learning flow. Ch01~Ch12 maintains unbroken prerequisite chains with no critical or major gaps. Pattern reuse (FSM, Driver, architecture updates) is the curriculum's strongest pedagogical feature. The emotional arc is well-designed with appropriate anxiety-relief cycles. Ready for Ch13+ continuation.
